title: Graphic Designer Job at the Vault Recruitment (TVR)
content:
The Vault Recruitment (TVR) - Our client is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Graphic Designer

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary


	Our client is seeking a creative and detail-oriented Graphic Designer to work onsite in Magodo, Lagos. The ideal candidate should have a strong visual sense and the ability to produce engaging, high-quality designs across digital platforms.


Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Design visually appealing graphics for social media, marketing materials, and campaigns
	Ensure all designs align with brand identity and guidelines
	Collaborate with marketing and content teams to deliver engaging creatives
	Manage multiple design projects and meet deadlines consistently
	Revise designs based on feedback and improve creative output


Requirements


	BSc/HND in a relevant field
	Minimum of 2 years of proven experience as a Graphic Designer (Portfolio required)
	Proficiency in Canva and Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator; After Effects is a plus)
	Strong creative thinking with a modern design approach
	Ability to work in a fast-paced, onsite environment
	Excellent attention to detail and time management skills
	Basic knowledge of digital marketing, branding, and content strategy is an added advantage.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "HR Generalist (HRG)",
  "description": "Mopheth Nigeria Limited is an organization charged with the goal of meeting the diverse needs of its growing customers from all walks of life. We began as a community Pharmacy in the year 1997, and through the years we have maintained our standard of being a renowned name in the Pharmaceutical industry in Nigeria. However, with respect to our vision consumables, cosmetics and skincare products, safe water production, grocery essentials, and a variety of baked goods. In addition, our 24 hours availability onsite and online in all our branches have equally ensured our clients can rely on us for immediate service at any hour of the day.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR Generalist (HRG)

Job Location:&nbsp;Lekki / Victoria Island (VI), Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Work Schedule: Monday - Friday | 8:00 AM &ndash; 5:00 PM (1 Hour Break)

Job Summary


	We are seeking a competent and proactive HR Generalist to manage day-to-day HR operations and support the organization in achieving its people and business objectives.
	The ideal candidate will handle recruitment, employee relations, performance management, and ensure compliance with HR policies and procedures.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage recruitment and selection processes (sourcing, screening, interviews, onboarding).
	Maintain and update employee records and HR documentation.
	Oversee attendance, leave management, and staff records.
	Support performance management and appraisal processes.
	Handle employee relations issues and disciplinary procedures.
	Ensure compliance with company policies and labor regulations.
	Coordinate staff training and development initiatives.
	Prepare HR reports and provide insights to management.
	Support payroll processes and staff welfare administration.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or related field.
	3&ndash;5 years of experience as an HR Generalist or similar role.
	Professional HR certification (e.g., CIPM, SHRM, or equivalent) is required.
	Strong knowledge of HR processes, labor laws, and best practices.
	Good interpersonal and conflict resolution skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (especially Excel).
	Strong organizational and multitasking abilities.
	Ability to work in a fast-paced, structured environment.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to&nbsp;hrmopheth@gmail.com&nbsp;using &quot;HR Generalist &ndash; VI&quot; as the subject of the mail.&nbsp;",

HR Generalist (HRG) Job at Mopheth Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Mopheth Nigeria Limited is an organization charged with the goal of meeting the diverse needs of its growing customers from all walks of life. We began as a community Pharmacy in the year 1997, and through the years we have maintained our standard of being a renowned name in the Pharmaceutical industry in Nigeria. However, with respect to our vision consumables, cosmetics and skincare products, safe water production, grocery essentials, and a variety of baked goods. In addition, our 24 hours availability onsite and online in all our branches have equally ensured our clients can rely on us for immediate service at any hour of the day.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR Generalist (HRG)
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Lekki / Victoria Island (VI), Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Work Schedule: Monday - Friday | 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM (1 Hour Break)

Job Summary


	We are seeking a competent and proactive HR Generalist to manage day-to-day HR operations and support the organization in achieving its people and business objectives.
	The ideal candidate will handle recruitment, employee relations, performance management, and ensure compliance with HR policies and procedures.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage recruitment and selection processes (sourcing, screening, interviews, onboarding).
	Maintain and update employee records and HR documentation.
	Oversee attendance, leave management, and staff records.
	Support performance management and appraisal processes.
	Handle employee relations issues and disciplinary procedures.
	Ensure compliance with company policies and labor regulations.
	Coordinate staff training and development initiatives.
	Prepare HR reports and provide insights to management.
	Support payroll processes and staff welfare administration.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or related field.
	3–5 years of experience as an HR Generalist or similar role.
	Professional HR certification (e.g., CIPM, SHRM, or equivalent) is required.
	Strong knowledge of HR processes, labor laws, and best practices.
	Good interpersonal and conflict resolution skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (especially Excel).
	Strong organizational and multitasking abilities.
	Ability to work in a fast-paced, structured environment.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to [email protected] using "HR Generalist – VI" as the subject of the mail.     

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

  "description": "Beebeejump International Limited is into the sales of Solar batteries, inverters, panels, and other accessories.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Assistant

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Responsibilities


	Assist with ensuring the smooth posting of job advert and recruitment process
	Review and maintain employee and employer records on staff.
	Manage junior staff and front desk operations
	Help implement new policies, procedures, methods, and systems
	Conduct fiscal reviews, and surveys.
	Responsible for the preparation of confidential documents and reports
	Ensure we follow all guideline stipulated in the labour law


Requirements and skills


	Proven experience as a Human Resource Assistant with at least Minimum of 1 year
	Knowledge of office management systems and procedures
	Working knowledge of Labour law
	Proficiency in MS Office (MS Excel and MS PowerPoint, in particular)
	Excellent time management skills and the ability to prioritize work
	Attention to detail and problem-solving skills
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills
	Strong organizational skills with the ability to multi-task
	Bachelor&#39;s Degree or equivalent experience
	Strong interpersonal, customer service, and communication skills.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVs to:&nbsp;hr@beebeejump.comusing &quot;Human Resouce Assistant&quot; as the subject of the email.",

Human Resource Assistant Job at Beebeejump International Limited




        
    

        
        Beebeejump International Limited is into the sales of Solar batteries, inverters, panels, and other accessories.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Assistant

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Assist with ensuring the smooth posting of job advert and recruitment process
	Review and maintain employee and employer records on staff.
	Manage junior staff and front desk operations
	Help implement new policies, procedures, methods, and systems
	Conduct fiscal reviews, and surveys.
	Responsible for the preparation of confidential documents and reports
	Ensure we follow all guideline stipulated in the labour law


Requirements and skills


	Proven experience as a Human Resource Assistant with at least Minimum of 1 year
	Knowledge of office management systems and procedures
	Working knowledge of Labour law
	Proficiency in MS Office (MS Excel and MS PowerPoint, in particular)
	Excellent time management skills and the ability to prioritize work
	Attention to detail and problem-solving skills
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills
	Strong organizational skills with the ability to multi-task
	Bachelor's Degree or equivalent experience
	Strong interpersonal, customer service, and communication skills.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CVs to: [email protected]using "Human Resouce Assistant" as the subject of the email.

  "description": "Nomba, launched in 2017 as &quot;Kudi.ai&quot;, is a chatbot that responds to financial requests on social apps. Nomba has since evolved to empower independent businesses to act as neighborhood banks offering basic financial services such as cash withdrawal, transfer and bill payments to all Nigerians, especially those in underserved communities. Today we process more than $500m in payments monthly.

With a vision to enable economic prosperity by supercharging businesses to run efficiently and meet their goals, nomba is making it easier for businesses to accept payments, make payments and manage their operations. With over 250 employees and more than 150,000 businesses on our platform across the country, we are creating a distinct gateway into the digital economy by providing businesses with access to everyday tools that simplify payments and drive their growth. With these easy-to-use tools, businesses of all sizes can now accept, process and manage payments online and offline while optimizing their operations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Banking Relationship Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos

Summary


	This role will be summarily responsible to build, manage and grow huge deposit mobilization from large corporate clients; drive deposits, lending, and cross-sell opportunities that align with our Organization&#39;s business banking growth strategy. Summarily manage non-retail business customers.


Responsibilities


	Drive growth in deposits, credits, and transactional volumes from clients in acquired portfolios.
	Mobilize large deposits from Big Segments viz: Shipping, Construction, Car Auto dealers, Real Estate, Oil &amp; Gas, Agriculture/Commodities, Health &amp; Pharma, Aviation Value chain, Construction, Crypto etc.
	Build and maintain strong, long-term client relationships to drive trust and retention.
	Drive consistent client inflows into client&#39;s Nombank account
	Ensure at least 20% of client deposits are retained as sustainable sitting balances.
	Work closely with product, compliance, and operations teams to ensure smooth onboarding and service delivery.
	Provide financial advisory and business solutions tailored to client needs (working capital, trade finance, payments, investment options, etc.).
	Partner with Product and Treasury teams to deliver innovative solutions that support client growth.
	Drive revenue via advisory fees, underwriting fees, fund management fees, etc.
	Ensure compliance adherence with CBN, AML/CFT, and regulatory policies.


Requirements
Educational and Experience:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Business, Finance, Economics, or related field. MBA or professional certification (CIBN, ACCA, CFA) is an advantage.
	5&ndash;7 years of experience in Fintech business or commercial banking with proven relationship management success.
	Strong understanding of credit analysis, SME financing, and market dynamics.
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and analytical skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office, CRM tools, and digital banking solutions
	Excellent negotiation, communication, and presentation skills.
	Leadership: leading deal teams, driving strategy, building products


Objective &amp; Key Result Areas:


	Client Acquisition: Onboard at least 50 active crypto clients within the first 3 months
	Inflows Growth: Achieve ?50B in monthly inflows in 6 months
	Sitting Balances: Ensure clients maintain at least 20% of deposits as average monthly balance
	AUM Growth: Grow aggregate Assets Under Management (AUM) to ?10B in 6 months.


Compensation Package


	Competitive base salary + 5&ndash;10% bonus
	Performance-based commissions and bonuses
	Health insurance, pension, and paid time-off benefit
	Learning &amp; development allowance
	Career progression opportunities in a challenging performance driven environment.

Business Banking Relationship Manager Job at Nomba




        
    

        
        Nomba, launched in 2017 as "Kudi.ai", is a chatbot that responds to financial requests on social apps. Nomba has since evolved to empower independent businesses to act as neighborhood banks offering basic financial services such as cash withdrawal, transfer and bill payments to all Nigerians, especially those in underserved communities. Today we process more than $500m in payments monthly.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



With a vision to enable economic prosperity by supercharging businesses to run efficiently and meet their goals, nomba is making it easier for businesses to accept payments, make payments and manage their operations. With over 250 employees and more than 150,000 businesses on our platform across the country, we are creating a distinct gateway into the digital economy by providing businesses with access to everyday tools that simplify payments and drive their growth. With these easy-to-use tools, businesses of all sizes can now accept, process and manage payments online and offline while optimizing their operations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Banking Relationship Manager

Job Location: Lagos

Summary


	This role will be summarily responsible to build, manage and grow huge deposit mobilization from large corporate clients; drive deposits, lending, and cross-sell opportunities that align with our Organization's business banking growth strategy. Summarily manage non-retail business customers.


Responsibilities


	Drive growth in deposits, credits, and transactional volumes from clients in acquired portfolios.
	Mobilize large deposits from Big Segments viz: Shipping, Construction, Car Auto dealers, Real Estate, Oil & Gas, Agriculture/Commodities, Health & Pharma, Aviation Value chain, Construction, Crypto etc.
	Build and maintain strong, long-term client relationships to drive trust and retention.
	Drive consistent client inflows into client's Nombank account
	Ensure at least 20% of client deposits are retained as sustainable sitting balances.
	Work closely with product, compliance, and operations teams to ensure smooth onboarding and service delivery.
	Provide financial advisory and business solutions tailored to client needs (working capital, trade finance, payments, investment options, etc.).
	Partner with Product and Treasury teams to deliver innovative solutions that support client growth.
	Drive revenue via advisory fees, underwriting fees, fund management fees, etc.
	Ensure compliance adherence with CBN, AML/CFT, and regulatory policies.


Requirements
Educational and Experience:


	Bachelor’s Degree in Business, Finance, Economics, or related field. MBA or professional certification (CIBN, ACCA, CFA) is an advantage.
	5–7 years of experience in Fintech business or commercial banking with proven relationship management success.
	Strong understanding of credit analysis, SME financing, and market dynamics.
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and analytical skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office, CRM tools, and digital banking solutions
	Excellent negotiation, communication, and presentation skills.
	Leadership: leading deal teams, driving strategy, building products


Objective & Key Result Areas:


	Client Acquisition: Onboard at least 50 active crypto clients within the first 3 months
	Inflows Growth: Achieve ?50B in monthly inflows in 6 months
	Sitting Balances: Ensure clients maintain at least 20% of deposits as average monthly balance
	AUM Growth: Grow aggregate Assets Under Management (AUM) to ?10B in 6 months.


Compensation Package


	Competitive base salary + 5–10% bonus
	Performance-based commissions and bonuses
	Health insurance, pension, and paid time-off benefit
	Learning & development allowance
	Career progression opportunities in a challenging performance driven environment.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Sterling Homes Limited&nbsp;is a private limited liability company incorporated in, 2010, by the Corporate Affairs Commission, Abuja, Nigeria, under the Companies and Allied Matters Act (CAMA) 1990. Our incorporation number is RC.894767. We are a real estate housing development, investment and allied Services Company.

We are poised to do real estate excellently, reinventing the way real estate business is managed in Africa and giving customer service delivery a new outlook. Our mission is to be one of the top ten home providers for individuals and corporate bodies in the next five years. We buy lands, finance real estate deals, build or have builders build projects, create, imagine, control and orchestrate the process of development from the beginning to the end.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Business Development Executive

Job Location:&nbsp;Ojodu Berger,&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Responsibilities


	Increase sales revenue through strategic planning
	Identify potential leads and them into customers
	Develop and implement plans that balance financial gain with customer satisfaction


Requirements


	A BSc / HND qualification&nbsp;in any field (Sales/Marketing background is an advantage).?
	Excellent communication and persuasive skills.
	Strong understanding of marketing and negotiation techniques?.


Compensation


	Competitive salary (N100,000 - N120,000 monthly)&nbsp;and benefits package
	Commission- 10% on all land sales and 5% on Housing sales target met
	HMO
	PENSION
	Hands-on training and mentorship in sales and marketing
	Performance based incentives and bonuses.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;careers@sterlinghomesltd.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note:&nbsp;For more information, contact the recruiter via: 09031216666.",

Business Development Executive Job at Sterling Homes Limited




        
    

        
        Sterling Homes Limited is a private limited liability company incorporated in, 2010, by the Corporate Affairs Commission, Abuja, Nigeria, under the Companies and Allied Matters Act (CAMA) 1990. Our incorporation number is RC.894767. We are a real estate housing development, investment and allied Services Company.

We are poised to do real estate excellently, reinventing the way real estate business is managed in Africa and giving customer service delivery a new outlook. Our mission is to be one of the top ten home providers for individuals and corporate bodies in the next five years. We buy lands, finance real estate deals, build or have builders build projects, create, imagine, control and orchestrate the process of development from the beginning to the end.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Executive

Job Location: Ojodu Berger, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Responsibilities


	Increase sales revenue through strategic planning
	Identify potential leads and them into customers
	Develop and implement plans that balance financial gain with customer satisfaction


Requirements


	A BSc / HND qualification in any field (Sales/Marketing background is an advantage).?
	Excellent communication and persuasive skills.
	Strong understanding of marketing and negotiation techniques?.


Compensation


	Competitive salary (N100,000 - N120,000 monthly) and benefits package
	Commission- 10% on all land sales and 5% on Housing sales target met
	HMO
	PENSION
	Hands-on training and mentorship in sales and marketing
	Performance based incentives and bonuses.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: For more information, contact the recruiter via: 09031216666.    

    

        Click here to Apply
